reason: The prompt cleanly targets mechanism-based differentiation: fluorinated-at-typical-levels shows no combustion/ignition-delay benefit, while SOC materially changes burning velocity, with an explicit boundary that ignition delay remains temperature-threshold governed. Decoys are not applicable in a short-answer format, but the item still strongly discriminates by forcing experts to avoid common overgeneralizations (e.g., “halon-like suppression” and “SOC eliminates ignition”), while remaining linguistically neutral and tightly bounded to the stated mechanisms.
